Ahead of the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, NBC is turning to an unlikely source to provide commentary.

Celebrity musician Snoop Dogg will serve as a prime-time analyst for this yearâs Olympic Games, NBC announced Sunday evening. The move follows his acclaimed streaming-only commentary of the 2020 Summer Olympics in Tokyo, provided alongside comedian Kevin Hart on Peacock.

âI grew up watching the Olympics and am thrilled to see the incredible athletes bring their A-game to Paris. Itâs a celebration of skill, dedication, and the pursuit of greatness,â the rapper said in astatement. âWeâre going to have some amazing competitions and, of course, I will be bringing that Snoop style to the mix. Itâs going to be the most epic Olympics ever, so stay tuned, and keep it locked.â

Snoop Dogg appeared in an advertisement for NBCâs Olympics coverage with several American athletes during theÂGreen Bay Packersâ 33-10 win over theÂMinnesota VikingsÂ on Sunday evening.

The opening ceremony for the Paris Olympics is currently scheduled to take place on July 26.

Patrick Andres is a staff writer on the Breaking and Trending News team at Sports Illustrated. He joined SI in December 2022, having worked for The Blade, Athlon Sports, Fear the Sword and Diamond Digest. Andres has covered everything from zero-attendance Big Ten basketball to a seven-overtime college football game. He is a graduate of Northwestern University's Medill School of Journalism with a double major in history .

title: NBC to Add Snoop Dogg as Reporter for Paris Olympics Coverage
description: The rapper will appear in prime time during the Games in July and August.
